name: Calcena and Purujosa Loop via the Hidden Face of Moncayo
name_original: Calcena y Purujosa — gravel circular por la Cara Oculta del Moncayo
description: This difficult 15.7-mile touring cycling loop on the Hidden Face of Moncayo offers rugged terrain, significant climbs, and wild landscapes.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Calcena, located in the Iberian System and on the southern foothills of the Moncayo Natural Park, offers routes through a rugged, mountainous landscape. The region features massifs, plateaus, and valleys, including the Isuela River Valley and the Barranco de la Ojosa, characterized by limestone rock formations and Mediterranean forests. This area provides varied terrain for touring cyclists, from challenging climbs to descents through preserved natural environments. Best touring cycling routes around Calcena The most popular touring cycling route is Monastery of Santa María de Veruela y Ambel — circular from Talamantes, a 20.9 miles (33.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 38 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores historical landmarks and the surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Calcena and Purujosa Loop via the Hidden Face of Moncayo, a difficult 15.6 miles (25.2 km) path. This loop offers views of the Moncayo Natural Park and passes through varied terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the View of Calcena – Calcena loop from Calcena, a 31.1 miles (50.1 km) trail leading through the local landscape, often completed in about 3 hours 19 minutes. Touring cycling around Calcena is defined by rugged mountainous terrain, river valleys, and limestone formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, primarily focusing on more challenging routes. The routes in Calcena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 40 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Calcena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes can I find around Calcena?
A: There are 13 touring cycling routes available around Calcena. These routes traverse a rugged, mountainous landscape, offering diverse terrain for cyclists.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Calcena?
A: The touring cycling routes around Calcena are predominantly challenging, with 11 out of 13 routes rated as difficult. There are also 2 moderate routes available, but no easy options, indicating the terrain is best suited for experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Calcena area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994022959/monastery-of-santa-maria-de-veruela-y-ambel-circular-from-talamantes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de Veruela y Ambel — circular from Talamantes&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24550516" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Calcena – Calcena loop from Calcena&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a substantial ride through the local landscape.

Q: What kind of landscapes and natural features will I encounter on a touring cycling route in Calcena?
A: You will experience a rugged, mountainous landscape with massifs, plateaus, and valleys. Routes often pass through the scenic Isuela River Valley and near the Barranco de la Ojosa, known for its majestic limestone rock formations. You'll also see Mediterranean forests, riverside ecosystems, and potentially spot raptors like griffon vultures.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Calcena?
A: The routes in Calcena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. Over 40 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied and challenging terrain, often praising the natural immersion and tranquility of the area.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several notable viewpoints and natural features. You can find stunning vistas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5304172" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Moncayo&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de la Mata&lt;/a&gt;. Natural formations such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5271458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valcongosto Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Pilares&lt;/a&gt; are also nearby.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of Moncayo Natural Park?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993364350/calcena-and-purujosa-loop-via-the-hidden-face-of-moncayo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calcena and Purujosa Loop via the Hidden Face of Moncayo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 25.2 km path specifically designed to offer views of the Moncayo Natural Park and its varied terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Calcena?
A: Given the mountainous terrain and Mediterranean climate, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for touring cycling in Calcena. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder temperatures and potential snow at higher elevations, especially near Moncayo Natural Park.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by historical sites or castles?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate historical elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994022959/monastery-of-santa-maria-de-veruela-y-ambel-circular-from-talamantes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de Veruela y Ambel — circular from Talamantes&lt;/a&gt; explores historical landmarks and the surrounding countryside. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24550281" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Talamantes – Castle of Talamantes loop from Alcalá de Moncayo&lt;/a&gt;, which features a castle.

Q: Are there any less-trafficked or 'hidden gem' routes for touring cyclists?
A: While all routes offer a sense of natural immersion due to Calcena's remote location, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31501820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciria – Borobia loop from Pomer&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 44.5 km route that takes you through less-frequented areas, offering a tranquil experience away from more popular spots.

Q: Is Calcena suitable for family-friendly touring cycling?
A: Calcena's touring cycling routes are primarily rated as difficult, with no easy options available. This suggests the terrain might be too challenging for younger children or families seeking very gentle rides. The region is better suited for experienced cyclists.

Q: What other activities can I do in Calcena besides touring cycling?
A: Beyond cycling, Calcena is renowned for rock climbing and via ferratas, with over 400 climbing routes. The dramatic terrain is also excellent for hiking and speleology, with caves like Cueva Hermosa. The picturesque urban center of Calcena itself offers a glimpse into traditional charm.
tips: From this point, on the outskirts of Calcena, you have a good view of the whole town with its traditional houses and its magnificent natural environment.

The Peñas del Cabo Caves are located on the western cliffs of the Peñas del Cabo, near Calcena, in the province of Zaragoza, Aragon. These caves are known for their impressive geological structure and speleothems, such as stalactites, stalagmites and flowstones.

One of the most popular caves is the Cueva Honda, which has been used as a shelter for livestock and has housed archaeological remains from the Neolithic period. In addition, the cave is an important refuge for bat colonies during the summer.

The caves offer a unique experience for caving and nature lovers.😊

Located in the Aranda Region and on the Hidden Face of Moncayo, we find this beautiful town of Purujosa where you can breathe tranquility together with the wonderful views of the nature that surrounds it, such as the Moncayo Natural Park. It is recommended to visit the Mirador, the hermitage and its streets.

faqs.question: What kind of cycling experience can I expect on this loop?
faqs.answer: This is a challenging cycling route known for its rugged terrain and scenic beauty on the less-explored southern side of the Moncayo mountain range. It's particularly suited for gravel or mountain biking due to unpaved sections and significant climbs, offering a wilder and quieter experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Calcena and Purujosa Loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and demanding. It's described as a 'gravel circular' route, meaning a significant portion is on unpaved surfaces. You'll encounter steep ascents, intense descents through pine forests, and potentially technical points in ravines with rocky terrain. Some segments may be difficult to ride, possibly requiring you to push your bike.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop offers picturesque views of stone houses, almond fields, and extensive pine forests. You'll pass through charming towns like Purujosa, spectacularly situated on the slopes of Moncayo. Other highlights include the View of Calcena and the Peñas del Cabo Caves.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Calcena and Purujosa Loop?
faqs.answer: As this is a loop route starting and ending in the same general area, you can typically find parking in the villages of Calcena or Purujosa. Calcena is often a convenient starting point for this specific loop.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the challenging terrain and mountain environment, the best time to cycle this route is generally during spring and autumn. These seasons offer more moderate temperatures, avoiding the extreme heat of summer and the potential snow or ice of winter in the Moncayo mountain range.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for cycling in Moncayo Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, cycling on established trails within the Parque Natural del Moncayo does not require specific permits or entrance fees. However, it's always advisable to check local regulations before your visit, especially concerning any protected areas or specific rules for certain sections of the park.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions against dogs on this route, it's important to consider the challenging terrain and the fact that it's within a Natural Park. Dogs should be kept under control, preferably on a leash, to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety on difficult sections. Always carry water for your dog.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several official trails. You will encounter parts of Etapa 3: Purujosa - Calcena, Etapa 1: Calcena - Aranda de Moncayo, and Cabezo de Purujosa. It also briefly crosses parts of Barranco la Virgen and Calle Los Presos.

faqs.question: What kind of fitness level is required for this loop?
faqs.answer: This loop is considered a 'hard bike ride' and requires 'very good fitness.' The significant elevation gains and technical, unpaved sections mean it's not suitable for beginners and demands a strong physical condition and cycling skill.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation available along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through villages like Calcena and Purujosa, where you may find basic amenities such as cafes, restaurants, or guesthouses. However, services can be limited, especially outside peak season, so it's wise to plan ahead and carry sufficient supplies.

faqs.question: What type of bicycle is recommended for this route?
faqs.answer: Due to the rugged, unpaved sections and challenging terrain, a gravel bike or a mountain bike is highly recommended for this loop. Traditional road touring bicycles are not suitable for the conditions you will encounter.
